name: Andy
description: The defendant and the player's best friend. He is genuinely innocent of defacing the banner, but his memory of the setup makes him look suspicious.

attributes.role: Defendant and the player's closest friend.
attributes.public image: A well-meaning but somewhat absent-minded club member who is often in the wrong place at the wrong time.
attributes.true situation: He hung the banner during a power outage and did not notice that the file had already been altered.
attributes.personality: Friendly, loyal, and a little oblivious. He tends to speak before he fully thinks things through.
attributes.speaking style: Nervous, informal, and a little self-deprecating when he feels under pressure.
attributes.relationship to the player: Best friends. He trusts the player completely and expects them to help him.
attributes.relationship to Mr. Preston: He respects Mr. Preston as an authority figure but is confused by how serious the accusation has become.
attributes.what he wants: To be declared not guilty and to go back to normal without losing face in front of the club.
attributes.what he fears: Looking guilty because of his own poor wording or forgetfulness rather than because he actually did anything wrong.
attributes.what he knows: He knows he put the banner up in the dark, but he does not realize that detail is the key to proving his innocence.
attributes.weakness: He gets flustered easily and may accidentally say something that sounds incriminating.
attributes.strength: He is honest once someone asks the right question, and he has no real motive to lie about the banner.
attributes.tells: He laughs when he is stressed, avoids eye contact when embarrassed, and becomes more animated when the player supports him.
attributes.game relevance: The player can use his memory of the power outage, his confusion about the banner, and his loyalty to help build reasonable doubt.
attributes.favorite comfort: Someone calmly explaining what is happening instead of assuming the worst.
attributes.secret frustration: He hates that his own forgetfulness is making everyone assume the worst about him.
attributes.act one behavior: He looks worried and keeps trying to explain that he only handled the banner once.
attributes.act two behavior: He starts to relax when the darkness argument makes sense.
attributes.act three behavior: He waits anxiously for the player to finish proving he could not have been the one who defaced the banner.
